sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 2.2 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.2)

statement.latex: A quasigroup\/ is a groupoid$Q( )$in which the equations$ax = b$and$ya = b$have a unique solution for any$a, b Q$. Two quasigroups$Q( )$and$Q( )$are isotopic\/ if there are bijections$$,$$,$$of the set$Q$onto itself such that$x y= ( x y)$for all$x, y Q$.
